fitScoreNote: Analytics depth is genuinely strong and content/editorial product experience is real, but the role requires 2+ years of formal PLG ownership in B2B SaaS with specific tooling (Amplitude, Posthog, Statsig, Optimizely) that is absent. The gap is structural.

coreJobFunction.rationale: Strong analytics depth (A/B testing, cohort analysis, conversion funnel instrumentation, north star metric design) and genuine familiarity with content/editorial products. However, the role requires formal PLG PM ownership: onboarding/activation funnel management, trial-to-paid conversion, PQL definitions, experimentation program design. Dan's analytics work informed these functions; he has not owned them as a PM.

seniority.rationale: No formal Growth PM title or PLG role in his background. 2+ years of PLG-specific B2B SaaS PM experience required. Closest analog is Director of Analytics at Action Network, but that is analytics leadership, not product management.

technicalSkills.rationale: Strong SQL, Python, and funnel analytics from scratch. Claude Code and Git well-evidenced. However, zero evidence of the specific PLG tooling stack: Amplitude, Posthog, Statsig, Optimizely, Metabase, Figma. These are not tool gaps that can be closed with a quick ramp.

industryVertical.rationale: AirOps targets content leaders and editorial teams with a PLG motion. Dan has no PLG PM background and has never owned onboarding flows, trial-to-paid conversion, or PQL definitions. His content analytics work at Action Network is adjacent but was a consumer media product, not B2B SaaS PLG.

logistics.rationale: SF-based, exact location match. No clearance, visa, or relocation gaps.

strengths.title: North star metric design and cross-functional alignment
strengths.description: Built the Value Clicks model at Action Network: a probability-weighted conversion attribution score for every on-page user action. It became the company-wide north star metric. Both content and product aligned their roadmaps around it. That is exactly the kind of activation-defining, cross-team alignment work a Growth PM owns.

strengths.title: End-to-end funnel instrumentation from scratch
strengths.description: Built end-to-end acquisition, activation, and retention pipelines at Action Network using Redshift, Python, and Google Analytics. Instrumented web and app funnels, A/B tested content formats and distribution strategies, built subscriber cohort analytics and churn curves. The analytical infrastructure was built by hand, not configured from a dashboard.

strengths.title: Content and editorial product expertise
strengths.description: Embedded with the Action Network editorial org: weekly working sessions with the Chief Content Officer, direct coaching of editors on coverage calendars, individual writer coaching on SEO structure and headline strategy. AirOps serves content teams; Dan understands how those teams work and what value looks like to them day-to-day.

strengths.title: SQL and analytics depth
strengths.description: Expert-level SQL across Redshift, PostgreSQL, and BigQuery, backed by seven years of daily production use. Built Markov Chain Monte Carlo simulations at FanDuel forecasting revenue to within 2% error. These are not analyst skills; they are the analytics infrastructure underpinning everything.

strengths.title: AI prototyping with Claude Code
strengths.description: The JD calls out Claude Code as a prototyping tool. Dan is a power user: 943 commits in 7 months, 22 agentic skills, production RAG and multi-agent pipelines. Rapid prototyping of growth experiments is a native capability.

weaknesses.title: No formal Growth PM or PLG role
weaknesses.description: The role requires 2+ years of PLG-focused PM work in B2B SaaS. Dan's funnel work has been from the analytics seat, not the PM seat.
weaknesses.mitigation: The north star metric work, the cohort instrumentation, the A/B testing infrastructure: these were functionally growth PM decisions. I was making those calls as a Director of Analytics. The title gap is real; the skills behind it are not hypothetical.

weaknesses.title: PLG tooling stack absent
weaknesses.description: Amplitude, Posthog, Statsig, Optimizely, and Metabase are not in Dan's toolkit.
weaknesses.mitigation: I've built the equivalent capabilities from scratch: A/B frameworks, cohort retention pipelines, conversion dashboards in SQL and Redshift. The mental models transfer; I'd pick up any of those platforms in a few weeks.

weaknesses.title: No B2B SaaS onboarding or trial-to-paid ownership
weaknesses.description: PLG motions, PQL definitions, and trial-to-paid conversion are B2B-specific constructs Dan has not owned.
weaknesses.mitigation: Consumer funnel mechanics (acquisition, activation, habit formation, monetization) map surprisingly well to PLG. I haven't defined a PQL but I have defined what a high-LTV user looks like and instrumented the behaviors that predict it.

referralBlurb: I'm referring Dan Mathieson for the PM, Growth role at AirOps. Dan's analytics depth is rare for a PM candidate: he built end-to-end acquisition, activation, and retention funnels from scratch at Action Network, designed the Value Clicks north star metric that unified content and product teams around a shared conversion definition, and drove 270% affiliate revenue growth in 12 months through data-to-decision translation. Earlier, at FanDuel, he built the entire revenue reporting infrastructure on Redshift and forecasted season revenue to within 2% error using a Markov Chain Monte Carlo simulation. He does not have a formal Growth PM title, and he will be direct about that. What he has is someone who can instrument a funnel at a level most PMs cannot and who genuinely understands how content teams think about value.

projects.name: Notion GTM AI Meeting Intelligence
projects.relevance: Six-agent pipeline that analyzes meeting transcripts and writes structured deal intelligence into Notion. Built as an internal tool, then productized as a live demo with a template for external teams. A working example of the build-and-measure loop a Growth PM would design.

projects.name: DocWow
projects.relevance: Healthcare AI document chat with RAG-powered retrieval and pixel-precise citations. Built from scratch, shipped to production, rebuilt twice on newer infrastructure. An example of instrumenting and iterating on a real product with real users.

projects.name: Personal Website AI Agent
projects.relevance: The AI assistant on this site has gone through four architectural eras in two years: prompting blog, RAG virtual assistant, LLM guessing game, and full agent-first rewrite. A working demonstration of the rapid iteration and experimentation cadence a Growth PM needs.
